reasoning: The question asks if the US inflation rate in 2025 will be strictly below 2.5% under a Trump presidency, resolving based on BLS data. The market traded at 7% probability on Nov 13, 2025. Recent BLS data shows that inflation accelerated to 3.0% year-over-year in September 2025. Additionally, the government has nixed the release of the October inflation report due to a historic shutdown, meaning the last official datapoint was 3.0%, and a new report dropping below 2.5% before the late 2025 horizon is highly mathematically improbable without unprecedented single-month deflation. Core CPI has also remained elevated. Thus, it is overwhelmingly unlikely the inflation rate reaches the <2.5% mark. I estimate the probability at 4%.
